paragraph: Single-family homes dominate Ridgefield Park's market, with 29 sales over seven months at a median of $620,000. About 55 percent of homes sold above asking, and the sale-to-list ratio sits at 1.01, which signals a competitive but balanced market where priced homes are moving without frenzied bidding wars. Median days on market is 33, giving buyers and sellers realistic time to negotiate. Multi-family properties are sparse in volume but show stronger seller momentum, with all five homes selling above list at a median of $635,000 and a median of 14 days on market. Condos and townhouses trail significantly in both volume and days on market, sitting at four sales with a 58-day median, suggesting this tier moves more slowly and buyers may find some leverage if they're shopping in this category. Co-ops round out the market at the entry point with three sales at a median of $199,000, though the sample size is too small to read reliable directional signals. If you're comparing tiers, the deciding factor isn't market heat. It's whether you want the space and grounds of a single-family, the rental income potential of a multi-family, the lower price point of a condo, or the lowest entry cost of a co-op.
